steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. The passage introduces a feature of the Aubrey/Maturin series (abrupt endings), then presents two opposing critical reactions to it. The correct answer must capture both the feature and the disagreement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'unusual structure...most complex books': The word "complex" never appears and neither book is ranked against the others. The passage does not argue complexity. ✗
  • Choice B — 'should have the literary renown of In Search of Lost Time': Proust is mentioned only as an analogy for a multivolume work, not as a benchmark the series should reach. ✗
  • Choice C — 'describe a characteristic...two differing viewpoints': Matches perfectly: the abrupt endings are the characteristic, and the word "But" signals the pivot to the second viewpoint. ✓
  • Choice D — 'remarkably entertaining despite flaws': Entertainment value is never mentioned. The second critic denies the endings are flaws, so "despite flaws" misrepresents the passage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
